mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least one month, the zero-lower-bound constraint becomes binding, signaling exhausted conventional monetary policy. This regime shift increases equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reassess growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 0.50% and remains there for 1+ month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% persists, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This decline produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously rises above 8.5%, the combination signals demand destruction and labor market stress. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as consensus downgrades earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) of at least 0.5% signals expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side dynamics. This expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month by at least 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO rises >= 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the combination signals monetary tightening-induced demand destruction in the housing sector. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction and related equity earnings compress.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing demand collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, the real policy rate becomes deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations expand under negative real rates.
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.0% and remains there for two consecutive months, labor market tightness signals wage pressure and potential inflation acceleration. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE < 4.0% persists for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline month-over-month, the combination signals demand-side stagnation. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as consensus downgrades full-year earnings guidance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
